channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Philippines Restricts Greek Animal Imports Due to Foot-and-Mouth Disease
item.description: The Department of Agriculture (DA) has announced a ban on imported live animals from Greece after the European country confirmed cases of foot-and-mouth disease in domestic sheep, particularly in Lesv...

item.title: Visayas Power Grid Faces Yellow Alert Due to High Demand and Plant Outages
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — The Visayas power grid was placed under a yellow alert for three hours yesterday after electricity reserves dipped below the required threshold. The National Grid Corp. of the Ph...

item.title: RSS conducts international outreach to address allegations over minority rights concerns
item.description: The Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS), a powerful Hindu nationalist organisation from which India’s ruling Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has ideological roots, has said it is engaging with foreign audi...

item.title: Discovery of Bicharracosaurus Dionidei Offers New Insights into Jurassic Sauropods
item.description: Paleontologists in Argentina have uncovered a new species of giant dinosaur, named Bicharracosaurus dionidei, providing fresh insights into Jurassic-era sauropods in the Southern Hemisphere. Measuring...

item.title: Health-focused climate messaging increases public support for government action
item.description: A recent international study by the Climate Opinion Research Exchange (Core), commissioned by the Wellcome Trust, shows that framing climate change as a health issue significantly boosts public concer...

item.title: Declining International Health Aid Poses Serious Challenges for Pakistan's Healthcare System
item.description: A recent report by think tank Tabadlab warns that Pakistan’s health sector is heading towards serious risks due to declining international aid. The study, titled 'Beyond Dependence: Understanding the ...

item.title: APC Screens Benue Assembly Aspirants, 59 Disqualified Over Document Issues
item.description: The Benue State chapter of APC don clear 86 aspirants and disqualify 59 people wey wan contest for the state House of Assembly primaries. This na result of screening wey the party House of Assembly Sc...

item.title: Ontario legislation restructures public water services, raising concerns over financialization and local accountability
item.description: In November 2025, the Ontario government enacted the Water and Wastewater Public Corporations Act (WCA) without public consultation, allowing provincial authorities to transfer local water and wastewa...

item.title: Doctors in Meru and Isiolo Announce Strike Over Unresolved Grievances
item.description: Doctors in Meru and Isiolo counties have served fresh 21-day strike notices, citing unresolved grievances that have pushed healthcare services to the brink. The Kenya Medical Practitioners, Pharmacist...

item.title: Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange Reports 320% Q1 2026 Growth Amid Rising Inflation and Investment Developments
item.description: For the first quarter of 2026, Tanzania’s financial markets showed strong performance, highlighted by a huge surge in trading activity on the Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange (DSE). Equity turnover skyroc...

item.title: Princess Nirgidma of Torghut: Life, Legacy, and Cultural Heritage
item.description: Princess Nirgidma of Torghut, born in 1907 in Tokyo, was a distinguished noblewoman whose life bridged traditional Mongolian nomadic culture and the cosmopolitan world of 20th-century Europe and Asia....

item.title: Maroondah Weather Update for Today
item.description: The article from the Herald Sun was intended to provide readers with a forecast for Maroondah's weather today. However, the content retrieved indicates that the website detected unusual traffic and bl...

item.title: Twelve Injured After Vehicle Rollover in Antipolo City, Rizal
item.description: Twelve individuals, including four minors, sustained injuries after their vehicle rolled over along Marcos Highway in Barangay Bagong Nayon, Antipolo City, Rizal on Monday afternoon. The vehicle invol...

item.title: Tanzania Development Bank to support climate-resilient farming loans
item.description: The Tanzania Agricultural Development Bank (TADB) is putting effort to boost farm lending that is friendly to climate change. Agriculture in Tanzania suffers big losses every year because of climate s...

item.title: Surge in Malawians Returning From South Africa Amid Rising Deportations and Safety Concerns
item.description: Malawi is currently experiencing an unusually high influx of citizens returning from South Africa, with immigration authorities reporting 1,347 arrivals over just 11 days between 1 and 11 May 2026. Th...

item.title: EAC Urged to Harmonise Policies to Boost Regional Trade to 40% by 2030
item.description: The East African Business Council (EABC) don call on all East African Community (EAC) member states make sure say dem align their fiscal policies and national budgets so regional trade go dey more smo...

item.title: Milly Vitale: Celebrating the Elegance and Legacy of a Post-War Italian Star
item.description: Milly Vitale (1933–2006) was a celebrated Italian actress whose charm and talent defined a golden era of post-war Italian cinema. Known for her delicate beauty and expressive presence, Vitale emerged ...

item.title: Investigation into NEET-UG Paper Leak Involving Coaching Centres and Technology
item.description: The NEET-UG examination, which is one of the most important medical entrance tests in India, faced a major paper leak scandal. According to preliminary investigations, several coaching centres were al...

item.title: Kenyan youth develop AI platform to independently track 2027 election results
item.description: In Kenya, Daniel Miringa, a young mechatronics graduate from Dedan Kimathi University, has developed a citizen-led platform called People's IEBC to independently tally results for the 2027 presidentia...

item.title: US Supports Feasibility Study for Sangley Point International Airport in Cavite
item.description: The United States Trade and Development Agency (USTDA) has approved funding for a feasibility study on the proposed Sangley Point International Airport (SPIA) in Cavite. The study aims to support the ...
